nodes.label: Equal Groups
nodes.description: Learning multiplication through equal groups, arrays, and times tables

nodes.label: What are Equal Groups?
nodes.description: Groups with the same number of objects in each group

nodes.label: Real-Life Examples
nodes.description: Packets of biscuits, rows of chairs, legs on tables, wheels on cars

nodes.label: Arrays
nodes.description: Objects arranged in equal rows and columns

nodes.label: Rows and Columns
nodes.description: Rows go across, columns go up and down; 3 rows x 4 columns = 12

nodes.label: Swap Property
nodes.description: 3 x 4 = 4 x 3 = 12; changing order does not change the answer

nodes.label: Link to Multiplication
nodes.description: Skip count by 3, four times: 3,6,9,12 = 4 x 3 = 12

nodes.label: Times Tables (2-10)
nodes.description: Lists of multiplication facts to memorise

nodes.label: Easy Tables
nodes.description: Tables of 2, 5, and 10 are easiest to learn first

nodes.label: Tricks
nodes.description: Table of 9: digits add to 9; Table of 5: ends in 0 or 5

nodes.label: Special Rules
nodes.description: Any number x 1 = same number; Any number x 0 = 0

nodes.label: Word Problems
nodes.description: Use multiplication to solve real-life problems about groups
